mnemonics: NON-LINEAR: Never Overlook Nonlinearity - Linearity Is Rare, Always Remember
keyPoints: Nonlinear models are used when the relationship between variables changes over different values or time.
Common techniques include nonlinear autoregressive (NAR) models and models with time-varying parameters.
Estimation often requires iterative numerical methods like Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE) or Nonlinear Least Squares.
commonMistakes: Assuming linearity by default without testing for nonlinear patterns in the data.
Misinterpreting volatility clustering (e.g., in ARCH/GARCH models) as a mean-based relationship.
Using overly complex nonlinear models that overfit the data instead of capturing the essential structure.
